purpose: The commit adds a check to prevent queuing commands when HCI_UNREGISTER is set, addressing a potential use-after-free issue.
The commit removes an unnecessary header file inclusion (asm/termios.h) from n_hdlc.c, as it's already included via linux/tty.h. This improves code maintainability.
The commit updates documentation to reflect changes in store metrics by adding 'N/A' entries, improving clarity for developers.
The commit fixes vma lock handling during split and unmapping by allocating new locks for duplicated vmas and properly freeing them to prevent sharing issues.

The commit adds documentation for AVDD and MICVDD supplies to the Realtek rt5682s device tree bindings, making them required. This improves code maintainability by ensuring proper hardware configuration.
The commit fixes a resource leak in the remove function by adding a missing iounmap() call, addressing an issue where resources were not properly freed.
The commit removes an unused 'work' field from the mlx5e_async_ctx struct, which is part of code cleanup without affecting functionality.
The commit changes the type of cfg80211_auth_request::key_idx from unsigned (u8) to signed (s8) to prevent warnings from static checkers when assigning -1 and to accommodate a range of -1..3.
The commit sorts CONFIG_SURFACE_PLATFORMS in RISC-V defconfigs without any functional changes.

The commit introduces a new function mcp251xfd_is_fd_mode() to check if the CAN controller is in FD mode or listen-only mode, replacing inline checks. This improves code readability and maintainability.
The commit modifies self-tests for RTC by skipping tests when the RTC device is not present, thus preventing test failures on systems without an RTC.
The commit removes a redundant assignment to the variable 'bytes' which was causing a dead code warning. This improves code quality by eliminating unnecessary code.
The commit decouples rcuscale from RCU Tasks to reduce kernel size and complexity.
The commit removes a duplicated phrase in a code comment, improving clarity without changing functionality.

The commit reverts changes to how clock tables are populated in the AMD display driver. It addresses an issue where using dcfclk-based tables caused a 'no read situation' when certain states were disabled, reverting until a better solution is implemented.
The commit adds documentation for Renesas RZ/V2L SoC's Mali GPU in device tree bindings.
This commit renames the struct pcie_port to dw_pcie_rp across multiple files in the DW PCIe core driver to maintain consistent naming conventions.

The commit fixes a clang -Wformat warning by correcting the format specifier in a debug message. It changes '%#06hx' to '%#06x' to properly handle integer promotion, improving code correctness and adhering to best practices.
The commit fixes a bug in the branch range check by casting the upper bound to long, ensuring correct signed comparison and preventing warnings.

The commit removes the HW_VAR_H2C_FW_P2P_PS_OFFLOAD case from SetHwReg8188EU() and replaces it with direct function calls to rtl8188e_set_p2p_ps_offload_cmd(). This simplifies the code by eliminating an unnecessary layer, improving maintainability.
The commit changes a symbol from global to static in arm64 code to fix sparse warnings.
The commit removes incorrect interrupt flags from device tree bindings to correct configuration.
The commit refactors array allocation in struct ctrl_ref by renaming variables and functions from 'dyn' to 'array', improving code clarity and maintainability.
The commit adds support for new hardware components (SoCs and PMICs) by updating the socinfo driver with additional models.
The commit updates the driver to recognize all possible chip cuts (A-P) by replacing a switch statement with a calculation, improving code efficiency and maintainability.
The commit removes the custom .is_enabled state from PRCMU clocks, simplifying the code by relying on the core's existing enable count mechanism.
The commit refactors the use of #ifdef CONFIG_XIP_KERNEL in RISC-V memory management code to reduce and group conditional directives, improving code readability and maintainability.
The commit enables QUP and GPI DMA hardware in the device tree configuration for sm6350-lena, allowing future addition of I2C nodes.
This commit improves code comments by clarifying descriptions, fixing typos, and adjusting coding style without changing functionality.
The commit fixes an infinite loop that occurs when failing to acquire the HW semaphore during resource release. It replaces the infinite loop with error logging and early exit.

The commit updates the terminology used for clocking in the pcm3168a driver by replacing 'master_mode' with 'provider_mode', aligning it with modern ASoC DAI format terms. This involves renaming variables and updating related code logic without introducing new functionality or fixing bugs.
The commit removes an unnecessary conditional definition of IS_SWAPFILE which was always defined, making the code cleaner.
The commit adds functionality to handle GTP Echo Requests by sending appropriate responses, ensuring compliance with the GTP specification.
The commit replaces cpumask_next_* functions with cpumask_first_* where appropriate, improving code effectiveness and adding a new function for finding the first zero bit in a cpumask.
The commit removes a call to 'dtim_assoc' IOVAR which was causing failures during STA roaming, thus preventing console error messages.
The commit adds support for a platform without an amplifier by introducing a new board configuration and updating the ACPI match table.
The commit adds a check to fail zero-copy sendmsg requests when the socket does not support it, thus preventing potential bugs.

The commit modifies the LaTeX style file to conditionally load xeCJK.sty only if both Noto Sans CJK SC font and xeCJK.sty are present. This prevents errors on systems where texlive-xecjk is not properly installed, addressing a specific bug.
The commit modifies the checkpatch script to ignore macros starting with 'case', preventing false error reports.
The commit refactors the simpledrm driver to use atomic helpers instead of simple-KMS helpers, improving code architecture and maintainability without introducing functional changes.
The commit adds a JPEG decode worker interface to support parallel execution of three hardware units on MT8195, improving performance and functionality.
The commit removes unnecessary VPRINTK() calls and the ata_scsi_dump_cdb() function, as their functionality is covered by tracepoints.
The commit adds support for entering zstate during extended vblank in freesync mode by calculating the extra time gained and using it if above a threshold, improving power efficiency.
The commit fixes a type mismatch in the hqd_destroy function pointer to resolve a CFI violation, addressing an existing bug.
The commit corrects a typo in the device tree bindings by changing 'reset' to 'resets' and adds 'additionalProperties: false', which prevents future configuration errors.
The commit silences an array-bounds warning in the ath9k driver when compiled with GCC 12 by adding a compiler flag to ignore such warnings.
The commit replaces pm_runtime_get_sync with pm_runtime_resume_and_get to simplify code in a crypto driver.
Refactor file installation and removal functions to improve code organization by moving them closer to related functionality.
The commit updates device tree bindings to split G1 and G2 decoder blocks into separate nodes with independent configurations.

The commit removes the IWL_DEVICE_BZ_COMMON macro and integrates its contents into the IWL_DEVICE_BZ macro. This is a code refactoring aimed at simplifying the configuration structure without introducing new functionality or fixing bugs.
